recalls.Description: HONEYJOY COMPANY is recalling several models of high chairs made from metal and plastic, featuring cloth seats in various colors. Each chair has a label indicating the model number and production date located on the top cover of the seat. The affected models include a range of convertible and foldable high chairs designed for infants and toddlers, which pose a risk of suffocation due to their incline angle exceeding 10 degrees, violating federal safety regulations.
recalls.Title: HONEYJOY Recalls Convertible and Foldable High Chairs Due to Suffocation Hazard and Regulatory Violations
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled high chairs immediately and contact HONEYJOY via email at honeyjoyrecall@hotmail.com or visit https://www.amazon.com/stores/page/A401562A-3E1E-43A1-8F23-3AAE28BA14FC?ingress=2&visitId=596d5ffc-9ec0-4624-baae-6aad8d0c70dd&store_ref=bl_ast_dp_brandLogo_sto&ref_=ast_bln for more information. They can also access www.amazon.com/stores/page/3AD84AC7-8A2C-402F-B205-F6D434FA6C53 and select "MORE" followed by "RECALL HIGH CHAIR".

Images.Caption: Recalled HONEYJOY 3-in-1 Infant Swing, High Chair and Booster Seat model BE10019US-GR in gray
Recalled HONEYJOY 3-in-1 Infant Swing, High Chair and Booster Seat model BE10019US-PI in pink
Recalled HONEYJOY High Chair model AD10025PI in pink

Recalled HONEYJOY 5-in-1 Convertible High Chair model BB4640BL in blue
Recalled HONEYJOY Convertible High Chair model AD10007BE in beige
Recalled HONEYJOY Foldable High Chair AD10014BE in beige with "Forest Friends" decoration.
Sewn-in Label with Model Number and Date of Manufacture

Retailers.Name: Available exclusively online at Amazon.com from April 2023 to August 2024, priced between $79 and $220.

Hazards.Name: Suffocation risk due to incline angle exceeding 10 degrees, violating CPSC regulations.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the high chairs, contact HONEYJOY for a full refund, deface the seat, and follow disposal instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: HONEYJOY recalls high chairs due to suffocation risks and safety violations. Immediate action required for affected consumers.

recalls.Description: Wayfair has issued a recall for Style Life Eleven baby loungers due to significant safety risks. These loungers, which feature a head pillow and can be folded into a portable handbag, have been found to violate federal safety regulations for infant sleep products. The loungers are available in various prints, including Dinosaur, Blue & White Crowns, Dotted Pink, Dotted White & Purple, Zoo Animals, and Gray & Brown Crowns. Only those purchased on or after June 23, 2022, are affected by this recall.
recalls.Title: Wayfair Recalls Style Life Eleven Baby Loungers Due to Suffocation and Fall Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled loungers immediately. For further assistance, contact Wayfair at 888-549-1625 from 8 a.m. to 8 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email AdvocacyTeam@wayfair.com, or visit https://www.wayfair.com/product_recall/ or www.Wayfair.com and click on 'Product Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Retailers.Name: Exclusively available online at Wayfair.com from June 2022 to September 2024, priced between $34 and $144

Distributors.Name: Bespoke Clothing Valley Inc., (Royalbelle), d/b/a Zoomie Kids, of China

Hazards.Name: Suffocation hazard due to thick sleeping pad and fall hazard due to low sides and lack of stand

Remedies.Name: Stop using the loungers and contact Wayfair for disposal instructions to receive a full refund

recalls.metaDescription: Wayfair recalls Style Life Eleven baby loungers due to suffocation and fall hazards. Immediate action required for refund.

recalls.Description: Mamibaby and Cosy Nation have issued a recall for their baby loungers, which feature a cloth cover, foam sleeping pad, and padded bumper. These loungers, marketed as 'baby loungers' and 'co-sleepers', come in various designs including Animal, Dinosaur, Elephant, Giraffe, and more. Notably, a tag labeled 'SLEEPING SET' is sewn inside, while the brand name is found on the exterior tag of most loungers, although some may lack these identifiers.
recalls.Title: Recall of Mamibaby and Cosy Nation Baby Loungers Due to Safety Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should reach out to MEIXIA Shop via email at recallemail@163.com for further assistance.

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com between June 2023 and June 2024, priced approximately $40 to $50.

Distributors.Name: Hangzhoumeixiajianzhushejiyouxiangongs D/B/A MEIXIA Shop, of China

Hazards.Name: The loungers do not meet federal safety standards for Infant Sleep Products, presenting risks such as low sides that fail to contain infants, a thick sleeping pad that poses suffocation dangers, potential falls from openings, and lack of a stand leading to falls from elevated surfaces.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the loungers immediately and contact MEIXIA Shop for disposal instructions to secure a full refund. MEIXIA Shop and Amazon are proactively reaching out to all known buyers.

recalls.metaDescription: Mamibaby and Cosy Nation recall baby loungers due to serious safety risks. Contact MEIXIA Shop for refunds and disposal instructions.

recalls.Description: Zazaba International has issued a recall for their Grão de Gente baby nests, which are made with a cloth cover, foam sleeping pad, and padded bumper. These products, available in various colors and designs, pose serious safety risks including suffocation, falls, and entrapment due to non-compliance with federal safety regulations. The baby nests feature tags that read 'Grão de gente'.
recalls.Title: Zazaba International Recalls Grão de Gente Baby Nests Due to Safety Hazards
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled baby nests immediately and reach out to Zazaba International Inc. at 855-425-4640, Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, or via email at recall@graodegente.com. More information can be found online at https://graodegente.com/pages/baby-nest-recall or by visiting https://graodegente.com and selecting 'Recall Notices' at the bottom of the page.
